rejects my non-linear objective for Hypatia but Convex.jl doesn't","user":{"login":"haansn08","id":6215916,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyMTU5MTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6215916?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08","html_url":"https://github.com/haansn08","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haansn08/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-28T19:45:03Z","updated_at":"2024-11-28T19:45:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I have a non-linear objective which is supported by the `Hypatia` solver (I think because my objective function can be rewritten to something linear and replaced with multiple "Logarithm cone" constraints, see equation (51a) of https://github.com/jump-dev/Hypatia.jl/wiki/files/coneref.pdf ). I know my program can be solved by `Hypatia` because `Convex.jl` seems to have no problem with it:

```jl
using Convex, Hypatia

winning_probabilities = Dict(
	[0,0] => 0.0134,
	[1,0] => 0.063,
	[0,1] => 0.4836,
	[1,1] => 0.44,
)
prices = [0.42, 0.8]

n = length(prices)
cash = Variable(1, Positive())
f = Variable(n, Positive())
objective = sum(p * log(cash + s'*(f./prices)) for (s,p) in winning_probabilities)
sum_one = cash + sum(f) == 1

problem = maximize(objective, sum_one)
solve!(problem, Hypatia.Optimizer)
```

However it seems JuMP doesn't like my objective and gives me an error:
```jl
using JuMP
model = Model(Hypatia.Optimizer)
@variable(model, cash, lower_bound=0, upper_bound=1)
@variable(model, f[1:n], lower_bound=0, upper_bound=1)
@constraint(model, cash + sum(f) == 1)
@objective(model, Max, sum(p * log(cash + s'*(f./prices)) for (s,p) in winning_probabilities))
# ERROR: The solver does not support an objective function of type MathOptInterface.ScalarNonlinearFunction.
```

I'm not sure if this is the right place to report this. If not, I apologize.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/JuMP.jl/issues/3896/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/JuMP.jl/issues/3896/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
